admin.inbox.maxConnections.description: Maximum number of concurrent connections to the server.
admin.inbox.maxRetries.description: Number of times to retry when a message fails.
admin.inbox.idleTimeout.description: IdleTimeout is the maximum time to wait for new activity on a connection before closing it and removing it from the pool.
admin.inbox.waitTimeout.description: PoolWaitTimeout is the maximum time to wait to obtain a connection from a pool before timing out. This may happen when all open connections are busy sending e-mails and they're not returning to the pool fast enough. This is also the timeout used when creating new SMTP connections.
admin.inbox.tls.description: TLS/SSL encryption, STARTTLS is commonly used.
admin.inbox.heloHostname.description: The hostname to use in the HELO/EHLO command. If not set, defaults to localhost.

admin.activityLog.help2: Use filters to narrow results by agent, event type, or date range.

admin.sharedViews.help: Shared views let you pre-filter conversations so agents can focus on relevant queues.

admin.macros.help: Combine multiple conversation actions into single-click macros.

admin.businessHours.help: SLA calculations are based on business hours configured here.
admin.businessHours.help2: Teams can override the global schedule with their own hours.

admin.sla.description: Define response and resolution time targets for conversations.
admin.sla.help: Configure SLA policies to set response, resolution and next response time targets.
admin.sla.help2: Breached SLAs trigger notifications to configured recipients.

admin.oidc.help: Configure single sign-on with one or more OpenID Connect providers.

admin.roles.description: Role-based access control for your agents.

admin.automation.title: Automations
admin.automation.description: Rules that run actions automatically based on events.

admin.domain.help2: Each domain requires proper DNS configuration for email routing.
admin.domain.empty: No domains configured yet. Add one to get started.
admin.domain.defaultDescription: Used as the initial suggestion for managed mailboxes.
admin.contactSegment.title: Contact Segments
admin.contactSegment.description: Organize contacts into dynamic groups based on filter rules.
admin.contactSegment.help: Segments are evaluated dynamically — any contact matching the filters belongs to the group.
admin.contactSegment.help2: Use segments to target specific groups in automation rules and shared views.
admin.contactSegment.empty: No segments yet. Create one to organize your contacts.
admin.contactSegment.filterDescription: Define which contacts belong to this segment.
admin.notification.helpDescription: Quick references to configure this channel safely and consistently.
admin.webhook.title: Webhooks
admin.webhook.description: Receive real-time HTTP notifications when events occur in the system.
admin.webhook.help: Configure webhooks to integrate CanalGov with external services.
admin.webhook.help2: Webhooks send HTTP POST requests when specific events occur.
admin.webhook.form.help: Configure the endpoint URL, events to subscribe, and an optional secret for signature verification.
admin.webhook.form.help2: Test your webhook after saving to confirm delivery is working correctly.
admin.inbox.title: Inboxes
admin.inbox.description: Manage the email channels for your workspace.
admin.inbox.help: Connect your email account (Google, Microsoft) or configure IMAP/SMTP manually.
admin.inbox.help2: Each added inbox creates a new email channel for receiving messages from contacts.
admin.customAttributes.title: Custom Attributes
admin.customAttributes.description: Define extra fields to capture additional data on contacts and conversations.
admin.customAttributes.help: Custom attributes let you record information beyond the default fields, like case type or government ID.
admin.customAttributes.help2: Use them in automation rules, filters, shared views, and segment conditions.
admin.template.help: Response templates are the default content base for service interactions.
admin.template.help2: Use global scope when the text serves the entire operation, or assign to a team for automatic prioritization in the composer.
admin.template.help3: Email layouts and notification templates are kept separate to avoid mixing reply content with technical structure.
admin.template.form.help: Write the template content using plain text or structured markup depending on the type.
admin.template.form.help2: Templates support variables like {contact_name} and {agent_name} for dynamic content.
admin.team.form.help: Set a name and optional description to identify this team in assignments and reports.
admin.team.form.help2: After creating a team, assign agents and configure business hours and SLA policy.
admin.agent.form.help: Fill in the agent name, email address, and role. An invitation will be sent to the email provided.
admin.agent.form.help2: The role defines which screens and actions this agent can access in the system.
admin.role.form.help: Create custom roles to restrict or expand agent access beyond the built-in defaults.
admin.role.form.help2: Permissions are granular — review each group carefully before saving.
admin.sla.form.help: Define time targets for first response, next response, and resolution.
admin.sla.form.help2: At least one time target is required. Alert rules are optional but strongly recommended.
admin.businessHours.form.help: Set the days and time windows when your team is available to respond.
admin.businessHours.form.help2: Teams without their own schedule inherit the workspace business hours.
admin.macro.form.help: Define a sequence of actions to execute with a single click during a conversation.
admin.macro.form.help2: Macros can send messages, assign agents, add tags, and change conversation status.
admin.sharedViews.form.help: A shared view is a saved filter visible to all agents or a specific team.
admin.sharedViews.form.help2: Shared views appear in the sidebar and help agents focus on relevant conversation queues.
admin.oidc.form.help: Register an OpenID Connect provider to enable single sign-on for your agents.
admin.oidc.form.help2: Agents can then log in with their institutional account instead of a local password.

admin.templates.description: Create reusable reply templates and email layouts.

admin.notification.officialCommunication: Centralize official letters, notices, and summons as internal service tickets with clear routing rules.
Workflow enabled to generate and route tickets according to the registered rules.
Workflow disabled or without routing policy defined.
Register the most common communication types to standardize administrative and legal triage.
Each rule can send the same ticket to one or more teams, based on the nature of the communication.
Set priority, initial status, and default inbox to reduce manual work.
The configuration saved here prepares the model for later automatic ticket creation integration.

admin.inbox.createLiveChatInbox: Create a live chat inbox for real-time customer support
admin.inbox.help.livechat: Create live chat widgets that can be embedded on your website for real-time customer support.
admin.inbox.livechat.preventReplyToClose: When enabled, customers cannot reply to closed conversations
admin.inbox.livechat.allowStartConversat: Allow users to start new conversations
Allow visitors to start new conversations
admin.inbox.livechat.chatIntroduction.de: Default: Ask us anything, or share your feedback.
admin.inbox.livechat.chatReplyExpectatio: Message shown to customers during business hours about expected reply times
admin.inbox.livechat.continuityEmailFoot: Reply directly to this email to continue the conversation.
admin.inbox.livechat.conversationContinu: When contacts go offline, replies will be sent from this email inbox. The contacts can continue the same conversation by replying to the email or in the chat widget when they return to your site.
admin.inbox.livechat.darkMode.descriptio: Enable dark mode for the chat widget
admin.inbox.livechat.directToConversatio: Skip the home screen and open directly into the user's latest conversation, or a new conversation if none exists

admin.inbox.livechat.features.fileUpload: Allow users to upload files in chat
admin.inbox.livechat.installation.authen: Then pass the signed JWT token to the widget initialization:
To identify logged-in users, generate a JWT on your server with the following payload structure:
Important: Sign the JWT using the HS256 algorithm with the secret key configured in the Security tab
Control the widget programmatically from your application.
admin.inbox.livechat.installation.instru: Paste it just before the closing `body` tag on every page where you want the live chat widget to appear

Prevent visitors from starting multiple conversations simultaneously

Show office hours after conversation is assigned to a team

admin.inbox.livechat.showPoweredBy.descr: Show "Powered by CanalGov" in the chat widget
admin.inbox.livechat.trustedDomains.desc: Specify your trusted domains and subdomains, one per line. Use an asterisk wildcard to trust all subdomains: *.example.com. Leaving this field empty will allow the widget to be embedded on any domain.
admin.inbox.livechat.blockedIPs.descript: Enter IP addresses or CIDR ranges to block, one per line (e.g., 192.168.1.0/24, 10.0.0.1). Blocked IPs will be denied access to the widget.
admin.inbox.helpCenter.description: Link a help center to inbox, articles from this help center will be used for AI assistant responses if enabled.
